channel.title: Javascript on Mike Ball
channel.description: Recent content in Javascript on Mike Ball

item.title: Testing JavaScript with node:test
item.description: Using Node.js's built-in test runner and assertion library to avoid third-party test dependencies.

item.title: Collecting Paginated HTTP API Response Data Using JavaScript's async/await
item.description: Collect all pages of data from a paginated HTTP API in JavaScript using async/await.

item.title: Mocking aws-sdk With Jest
item.description: How can the aws-sdk be mocked using jest?

item.title: How to Test Google Polymer elements on Travis CI
item.description: How to run a headless Firefox on Travis CI via Xvfb

item.title: JavaScript Modules
item.description: A few notes on JavaScript modules.

item.title: Using Google Spreadsheets and Tabletop.js as a Web Application Back-end
item.description: How to use Tabletop.js as your JavaScript app's datastore.

item.title: Using the civic-info Node.js Module to Get Voter and Election Info
item.description: A quick tutorial in using a simple NPM module I built.

item.title: Deploying an Express App to Heroku
item.description: A beginner's tutorial in deploying a Node.js app to Heroku.

item.title: Testing Node.js with Mocha, Expect.js, and Nock
item.description: A technique for mocking HTTP requests in your Node.js tests.

item.title: Using Mocha to Test a Node.js Server
item.description: Using Node.js's fork to write tests against a server.

item.title: Fluent 2012 Notes: Nicholas Zakas on Maintainable JavaScript
item.description: My notes on Nichalas Zakas' Fluent 2012 talk.

item.title: How to Make Testable Private Methods in JavaScript
item.description: A controversial technique for writing unit tests against private methods.

item.title: JavaScript Design Patterns
item.description: My notes on Addy Osmani's JavaScript design patterns ebook.

item.title: free103point9
item.description:
Gabe Farrell and I developed this site for the New York-based transmission arts advocacy organization free103point9.

The site is built on Django. In addition to brochuring free103point9, the site also serves as a transmission arts digital archive, hosts an event calendar, and houses free103′s online radio station.
